heroDescription: Global Logistics Software • ~17,000 Customers • 174 Countries • e2open Acquired
heroCompanyDescription: WiseTech Global (ASX: WTC) is the world's leading logistics technology company, operating the CargoWise platform that serves as the dominant operating system for international freight forwarding, customs brokerage, and supply chain management. The August 2025 acquisition of e2open for US$2.4bn transformed WiseTech from a pure-play logistics SaaS company into a broader supply chain platform. The company now serves approximately 17,000 organisations across 174 countries. Founder Richard White holds 36% of issued capital and serves as Executive Chairman.

skew.rationale: 58% combined weight on upside scenarios (N1 Franchise Intact at 28% + N2 Restructuring Recovery at 30%). Evidence-weighted price A$59 vs market A$47.10. 1H26 results confirmed e2open tracking within guidance and delivered a restructuring catalyst the market was not pricing. Primary downside scenario (covenant breach) materially weakened.

verdict.text: WiseTech is trading at A$47.10, 25% below the evidence-weighted price of approximately A$59. The 1H26 results confirmed what the market expected: e2open tracking within guidance, blended margins diluted to 38%, the covenant navigable. Then management announced something the market was not pricing: a 2,000-person restructuring representing US$160–240M of annualised cost savings from FY27, on top of the US$50M synergy target achieved 18 months ahead of schedule. The combined cost-out programme is three to four times larger than what was in guidance. The one red flag: CargoWise organic growth at 9% versus the 14% guided floor. If this is transitional (Value Pack timing), it is noise. If structural, the franchise is worth less. August tells us.

featuredRationale: 25% gap widened by 1H26 results. Restructuring + synergy beat create credible path to 48-50% margins by FY28. CW organic growth at 9% is the one red flag. August FY26 results are the discriminator.

paragraphs: Before these results, we built a probability-weighted framework around four scenarios for WiseTech's future, ranging from franchise intact and the e2open acquisition works through to the acquisition triggers a reflexive unravelling. We assigned probabilities to each, valued each one, and computed an evidence-weighted price: what you would pay if you believed the evidence rather than the market. That pre-results price was A$54.56 against a market price of A$47.10, a 16% gap to the upside.

The 1H26 results confirmed what the market already expected: e2open is tracking within guidance, margins are diluted by the acquisition, and the covenant is navigable. No surprises there. But management also announced something we did not model and the market was not pricing: a 2,000-person restructuring, a 50% headcount reduction across product, development, and customer service, representing US$160–240M of annualised cost savings from FY27. This sits on top of the US$50M synergy target that was achieved 18 months ahead of schedule.

Yesterday the market was pricing a slow, multi-year margin grind back toward the mid-40s. Today, there is a credible path to 48–50% EBITDA margins by FY28, a margin recovery that is three to four times larger and eighteen months faster than what was in the price. Our revised evidence-weighted price is approximately A$59. The gap to market has widened from 16% to 25%.

The one concern: CargoWise organic growth at 9% versus the 14% guided floor. If this is transitional (Value Pack timing disruption with 95% of customers migrated in December 2025), it is noise. If it is structural, the franchise is worth less and every scenario's implied price declines. The FY26 full-year results in August are the definitive test.
For anyone positioned to act on a 25% gap with a 6–12 month view, the case for upside coming out of these results is the strongest it has been since we initiated coverage.

identity.overview: WiseTech Global (ASX: WTC) operates CargoWise, the dominant global platform for freight forwarding, customs brokerage, and supply chain management. Founded in 1994 by Richard White, the company serves approximately 17,000 organisations across 174 countries. The August 2025 acquisition of e2open for US$2.4bn transformed WiseTech from a pure-play logistics SaaS company into a broader supply chain platform. 1H26 total revenue of US$672M reflected five months of e2open contribution (US$249.4M) alongside CargoWise revenue of US$372.4M (+12%, 9% organic). Richard White holds 36% of issued capital and returned as Executive Chairman in February 2025 following a governance crisis that saw four board members depart. ASIC investigation into the founder remains unresolved.

hypotheses.title: N1: Franchise Intact, Acquisition Works
hypotheses.statusText: Weakened: CW organic growth at 9% vs 14% guided
hypotheses.description: CargoWise keeps compounding at 14%+ organic growth. e2open integration succeeds. Margins recover to 50% by FY28. The bull case requires both the franchise and the acquisition to work simultaneously. Three-year path delivers FY28 EBITDA of approximately US$950M at 50%+ margins.
requires: CargoWise organic growth reaccelerates to 14%+ in 2H26 and sustains
e2open integration delivers full synergy programme beyond the US$50M already achieved
EBITDA margins recover to 50% by FY28 through combined organic growth and cost-out
Governance risk does not impair institutional confidence or capital allocation
LGFF count continues expanding and Value Pack drives net revenue retention above 110%
supporting: 13 unanimous Buy ratings; consensus target A$101.21 (105% upside from pre-results price)
US$50M synergy target achieved 18 months early: management can execute on cost
CargoWise gross margin flat at 87% ex-e2open: franchise economics intact underneath the blended dilution
LGFF count at 57, expanding; 95% of customers on Value Pack pricing model
contradicting: CW organic growth at 9% vs 14% guided floor: the single most important metric weakened
Existing customer growth fell sharply: US$20.3M vs US$44.7M prior period
Governance unresolved: White's 36% control, ASIC investigation active, no board reconstitution
No active accumulation at current levels despite 50%+ discount to broker targets
hypotheses.distinguishingFeature: N1 is the only scenario where both the franchise and the acquisition work. It requires the 9% organic growth to be transitional (Value Pack timing) rather than structural. If CW growth reaccelerates to 12%+ in 2H26, N1 regains the weight it lost today. If it stays at 9%, the compounder thesis that justifies the premium multiple in this scenario is damaged.
hypotheses.uniqueVulnerability: The 9% organic growth. Every other data point in the 1H26 results was either in line or better than expected. This was worse. The distinction between Value Pack timing disruption and genuine franchise deceleration cannot be resolved before August.

hypotheses.title: N2: Restructuring Recovery
hypotheses.statusText: New Scenario: Upgrade story not yet priced
hypotheses.description: Same Integration Tax starting point as N3, but the aggressive cost-out programme announced on 25 February 2026 accelerates margin recovery. US$50M synergies (achieved 18 months early) plus 2,000-person restructuring (US$160–240M annualised savings) combine to push FY28 EBITDA to US$800–850M at 48–50% margins. This is the upgrade story the market has not priced.
requires: Restructuring delivers 60%+ of gross savings (US$96–144M incremental EBITDA from FY27)
Product quality, release cadence, and customer experience are not impaired by 50% headcount reduction
AI tools replace human coding and customer service functions as management claims
July 2026 covenant test passes at ≤3.3× removing tail risk
supporting: US$50M synergy target delivered 18 months ahead of schedule: management track record on cost execution is now established
2,000-person restructuring across product, dev, and customer service: US$160–240M gross annualised savings at US$80–120K blended cost per role
At 60% realisation, US$96–144M incremental EBITDA from FY27: transforms the margin trajectory from mid-40s grind to 48–50% by FY28
Covenant headroom improved by +0.3–0.5×: synergy beat and restructuring savings add meaningful buffer to July test
FY28 EBITDA path US$800–850M: three to four times larger cost-out than guidance implied
contradicting: Cutting 50% of product and development headcount is aggressive: if AI tools are not ready, product quality or release cadence could suffer
FY26 impact guided as neutral: execution costs offset savings in the near term; no visible margin improvement until FY27
Customer service headcount reduction could impair implementation quality for new LGFF customers
Restructuring cost estimates are management-sourced; independent verification will not be available until FY27 results
hypotheses.distinguishingFeature: N2 did not exist before 25 February 2026. It was created by a single day's disclosure. The Integration Tax scenario (N3) assumed FY28 EBITDA of approximately US$714M at a 44.5% margin. Add US$100–140M of restructuring savings and you reach US$800–850M at 48–50% margins. That is the difference between a stock that goes nowhere for three years and one that re-rates as the margin recovery becomes visible in FY27 reporting.
hypotheses.uniqueVulnerability: The AI replacement thesis. Management frames the restructuring as AI-driven transformation. If the AI tools are not ready, the company has eliminated 2,000 people across product development and customer service without a replacement capability. The risk is real and unquantifiable until the next product cycle. No external observer can assess whether WiseTech's internal AI tooling can replace the output of 2,000 engineers and service staff.

hypotheses.title: N3: Integration Tax
hypotheses.statusText: Base Case: Confirmed but exit path created
hypotheses.description: The e2open acquisition does not destroy value but does not create much either. Margins grind slowly back toward the mid-40s over three years. CargoWise franchise intact but growing at single digits. Management attention divided between integration and organic growth. Stock price stagnates as the market waits for evidence of either recovery (shifting to N2) or deterioration (shifting to N4). Three-year path delivers FY28 EBITDA of approximately US$714M at 44.5% margins.
requires: Restructuring underdelivers: less than 50% of gross savings realised due to execution complexity
CargoWise organic growth remains at 9–11%, below the 14% guided floor
Blended margins recover slowly from 38% toward mid-40s, not the 48–50% in N2
supporting: 1H26 blended EBITDA margin of 38%: e2open dilution confirmed as expected
CW organic growth at 9%: below guidance, consistent with integration distraction
Existing customer growth fell sharply from US$44.7M to US$20.3M: management attention may be splitting
Underlying NPAT flat at US$114.5M (+2%): integration tax at work, earnings going nowhere
contradicting: US$50M synergies achieved 18 months early: management executing faster than base case assumes
Restructuring programme announced: if even partially successful, N3 migrates toward N2
CargoWise gross margin flat at 87%: core franchise economics not impaired by integration
hypotheses.distinguishingFeature: N3 and N2 start from the same position. The difference is execution on the restructuring programme. N3 assumes 50% or less of gross savings materialise. The FY27 half-year results become the discriminating event: if blended EBITDA margins are tracking above 42%, weight migrates from N3 to N2. Below 40%, N3 solidifies as the dominant scenario.
hypotheses.uniqueVulnerability: N3 is inherently unstable. It describes a transition state, not an equilibrium. Over 12–18 months, the evidence will push weight either toward N2 (restructuring works) or toward a worse version of N3 where integration failure becomes structural. The vulnerability is that N3 resolves itself rather than persists.

hypotheses.title: N4: Reflexive Unravelling
hypotheses.description: e2open collapses, the July 2026 covenant test is breached at 3.5×+ leverage, lenders demand repayment, and WiseTech is forced into an emergency equity raise at a steep discount. The worst case. Today's results have weakened it materially: synergy beat and restructuring savings add 0.3–0.5× of additional covenant headroom.
requires: e2open revenue deteriorates beyond the current annualised ~US$550M guidance
Restructuring execution fails completely with no realised savings
Credit markets tighten or lenders refuse waiver
supporting: Pre-results covenant headroom was razor-thin: approximately 0.1×, one bad quarter from breach
US$2.4bn acquisition debt at ~2.9× leverage remains structurally elevated
Statutory NPAT declined 36% to US$68.1M, reflecting US$66M interest + US$38M acquired amortisation
contradicting: US$50M synergies add +0.3× headroom: achieved, not projected
Restructuring savings add +0.1–0.2× in 2H26: partial benefit begins before July test
Combined headroom now +0.3–0.5×: covenant pass at ≤3.3× takes debt crisis off the table
e2open tracking within guidance: annualised ~US$550M, no deterioration signal
hypotheses.distinguishingFeature: N4 was a 20% probability scenario before these results. It lost 8 percentage points in a single day because the two buffers against covenant breach (synergies achieved, restructuring announced) both materialised simultaneously. If the July test comes in at 3.3× or below, N4 is effectively dead and the market can stop pricing the risk of a capital raise.
hypotheses.uniqueVulnerability: An exogenous shock. N4 now requires something management cannot control: a credit market freeze, a counterparty failure that disrupts refinancing, or e2open revenue collapse. The probability is low precisely because the endogenous risks (execution, synergies) have been addressed. Residual N4 weight reflects tail risk, not central tendency.

narrative.theNarrative: The market narrative has shifted three times in 18 months. Until October 2024, WTC traded at A$127 as one of Australia's most respected technology companies. The governance crisis (founder conduct allegations, mass board departure, White's return as Executive Chairman) drove the stock to A$47, a 63% decline. The August 2025 e2open acquisition for US$2.4bn added integration risk on top of governance risk. Now the 1H26 results have introduced a third narrative: the restructuring recovery. Management delivered US$50M synergies 18 months early and announced a 2,000-person headcount reduction representing US$160–240M of annualised savings. The market was pricing a slow margin grind. The evidence now supports a credible path to 48–50% EBITDA margins by FY28. One number complicates the picture: CargoWise organic growth at 9% versus the 14% guided floor. If this is structural deceleration rather than Value Pack transition noise, every upside scenario is worth less.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$47.10
priceImplication.content: At A$47.10, the market embeds roughly 55–60% probability on the Integration Tax and Unravelling scenarios (N3+N4) and 40–45% on the upside scenarios. The evidence assigns 58% to upside (N1+N2) and 42% to downside (N3+N4). The disagreement centres on whether the restructuring programme creates a credible margin recovery or whether it is a distraction that weakens the product. If CW organic growth reaccelerates to 12%+ in 2H26 and any margin improvement appears in the FY26 full-year result, N2 (Restructuring Recovery) gains 5–8 percentage points and the evidence-weighted price rises to A$63–66. If CW organic growth stays below 10% and margins remain at 38%, N3 solidifies and the evidence-weighted price falls to A$51–54.

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence is sharply bifurcated. Financial execution metrics are strong: US$50M synergies achieved 18 months early (objective, auditable), e2open tracking within guidance (statutory), CargoWise gross margin flat at 87% ex-e2open (auditable). The restructuring announcement is management-sourced and forward-looking. The CW organic growth number (9% vs 14% guided) is the single data point that cuts against the upside case and it is independently verifiable against LGFF count and revenue disclosures. Governance indicators remain uniformly negative: no board reconstitution, ASIC investigation active, founder control intact. The strongest evidence domains are statutory filings and executed synergies; the weakest are forward guidance on restructuring and AI replacement thesis.
narrative.narrativeStability: LOW-MEDIUM. Three catalysts within 6 months: strategy/board updates (ongoing), July 2026 covenant test, August 2026 FY26 full-year results. The restructuring programme introduces a new variable (execution risk) that will become observable in FY27 margins. CW organic growth in 2H26 is the binary discriminator between N1/N2 and N3. High sensitivity to a single metric.

qualityOfEarnings.inflectionDependency: The 1H26 result is mechanically complicated by the e2open acquisition (consolidated from August 2025, so only 5 months of contribution). Total revenue of US$672M (+76%) is acquisition-driven. The diagnostic number is CargoWise organic revenue growth: 9% versus 14% guided and 17% achieved in FY25. Existing customer revenue contribution fell from US$44.7M to US$20.3M. New customer growth improved to US$10.1M (vs US$8.6M). The Value Pack pricing model, launched December 2025 with 95% migration, may have disrupted the recognition profile.
qualityOfEarnings.threePhaseArc: Phase one: the pre-crisis compounder. WTC traded at 50–60× earnings as a high-growth SaaS company with 53%+ EBITDA margins. Phase two: the governance collapse. Stock fell 63% as the market repriced governance risk, then the e2open acquisition added leverage and integration risk. Phase three begins now. The 1H26 results signal that the acquisition is not destroying value (e2open within guidance) and the cost-out programme is larger and faster than expected. The question is whether the company transitions from phase two to a new compounding phase or remains trapped in integration purgatory.
qualityOfEarnings.roeFlatterers: Statutory NPAT of US$68.1M (-36%) is mechanically depressed by US$66M of interest expense on acquisition debt and US$38M of acquired intangibles amortisation. Underlying NPAT of US$114.5M (+2%) strips these out but reveals flat earnings: the integration tax at work. EBITDA of US$252.1M (+31% reported, +7% organic) is the more diagnostic metric, showing the franchise is generating cash above the acquisition noise.
qualityOfEarnings.balanceSheetFragility: The US$2.4bn acquisition debt at ~2.9× leverage is the structural risk. Pre-results covenant headroom was approximately 0.1× (razor-thin). The synergy beat and restructuring savings have improved this by +0.3–0.5×, making a July 2026 covenant breach unlikely but not impossible. A pass at ≤3.3× effectively removes the capital raise risk; a tight pass at 3.4–3.5× preserves some tail risk.

evidence.intro: Ten evidence domains assessed across the epistemic hierarchy, updated for 1H26 results released 25 February 2026. Evidence ranked: Audited Statutory > Objective Data > Independent Sources > Consensus > Motivated > Noise.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: Results in line; restructuring is the material new information. Management framed the 1H26 result as confirming e2open integration on track. Revenue guidance reaffirmed. The restructuring was positioned as AI-driven transformation, cutting approximately 2,000 roles (50% of product, development, and customer service). Management explicitly attributed US$160–240M of annualised cost savings from FY27. CargoWise organic growth of 9% was acknowledged below the 14% guided floor, attributed to Value Pack transition timing.
cards.tension: The restructuring framing as AI-driven transformation may overstate the readiness of AI tools to replace 2,000 people. Management's attribution of the growth shortfall to Value Pack timing is plausible but self-serving. The distinction between timing disruption and structural deceleration cannot be resolved until August.
cards.source: WTC 1H26 Results Presentation, 25 Feb 2026; Management commentary

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: 1H26 financials confirm integration tax, not value destruction. Total revenue US$672M (+76%, acquisition-driven). CargoWise revenue US$372.4M (+12%, 9% organic). e2open revenue US$249.4M (5 months, annualised ~US$550M within guidance). EBITDA US$252.1M (+31%), margin 38% (blended dilution from e2open). Statutory NPAT US$68.1M (-36%, mechanical: US$66M interest + US$38M amortisation). Underlying NPAT US$114.5M (+2%, flat). US$50M annualised synergies achieved January 2026.
cards.tension: The headline numbers mask a bifurcation: CargoWise franchise economics are intact (87% gross margin ex-e2open) while blended margins are diluted. Underlying NPAT is flat, confirming the integration tax. The synergy achievement is the strongest positive signal.
cards.source: WTC 1H26 Appendix 4D; ASX Filings; Results Presentation

cards.title: 3. Broker & Consensus Estimates
cards.finding: 13 unanimous Buy ratings with A$101 average target, but targets are stale. The 105% implied upside from broker consensus is one of the widest gaps on the ASX. However, most targets predate both the February 2025 board mass departure and the e2open acquisition. The gap between broker confidence and market price reflects the market's governance discount, which sell-side models do not formally incorporate. No active manager has filed an accumulation notice despite the 50%+ discount to consensus.
cards.tension: Unanimous Buy at 105% implied upside would normally be a strong signal. The absence of institutional accumulation despite this discount suggests the market disagrees with sell-side analysis on a dimension (governance) that consensus models do not capture.
cards.source: Bloomberg consensus; ASX substantial holder notices

cards.title: 4. Competitor & Industry Data
cards.finding: CargoWise remains dominant; no competitor has capitalised on the crisis. Key competitors (Descartes, BluJay/e2open legacy, Oracle TMS) lack CargoWise's integrated single-platform approach. Multi-year switching costs (3–5 years for enterprise logistics customers) protect the installed base. No evidence of competitive incursion during the 18-month governance crisis. The e2open acquisition consolidates supply chain planning capabilities that no competitor combines with freight execution at WiseTech's scale.
cards.tension: Product dominance is necessary but not sufficient. The question is whether great products inside poorly governed companies can sustain value accretion to minority shareholders over time. The academic literature suggests they often cannot.
cards.source: Industry analysis; competitor filings (Descartes FY25, Oracle); customer switching cost research

cards.title: 5. Economic & Regulatory Data
cards.finding: Logistics digitisation tailwind intact; trade recovery supports volumes. Global trade volumes recovering (WTO forecast +3.3% for 2025). Logistics digitisation penetration remains below 30% in most markets, providing a long-duration structural tailwind. Cross-border e-commerce growth supports freight forwarding software demand.
cards.tension: Macro tailwinds help all logistics software companies, not WiseTech specifically. If CW organic growth is decelerating from mid-teens to single digits in a supportive macro environment, the franchise may be losing share rather than riding a wave.
cards.source: WTO Global Trade Outlook 2025; Industry digitisation surveys

cards.title: 6. Price Action & Market Behaviour
cards.finding: Stock down 59% from highs; 1H26 results triggered volatile but constructive response. WTC fell from A$127 (October 2024) to A$47 (February 2026) through three phases: governance shock (-21% in one week), sustained institutional selling (death cross July 2025), and capitulation selling (break below A$70 December 2025, below A$50 February 2026). Mean reversion indicators show the stock at the 6th percentile of its 52-week range.
cards.tension: Extreme oversold conditions can persist in governance crisis stocks. The 63% decline may reflect appropriate repricing of structural governance risk rather than overshooting. The absence of institutional accumulation suggests the market views the discount as warranted, not as an opportunity.
cards.source: ASX market data; WTC price and volume history

cards.title: 7. Academic & Structural Research
cards.finding: B2B switching costs confirm moat; founder entrenchment literature is cautionary. Academic research on B2B software switching costs confirms 3–5 year lock-in for enterprise logistics platforms, supporting the CargoWise moat thesis. Separately, founder control literature shows that forced CEO departures followed by founder returns are associated with negative long-term shareholder outcomes in approximately 60% of cases. Concentrated ownership above 30% makes external intervention (activist campaigns, hostile bids) structurally difficult.
cards.tension: The two bodies of research pull in opposite directions. Switching costs protect the franchise (supporting N1/N2). Founder entrenchment risks impair value accretion to minorities (supporting N3/N4). They can coexist: a great product inside a poorly governed company. The question is which force dominates over time.
cards.source: B2B switching cost literature; corporate governance and founder control research

cards.title: 8. Media & Public Commentary
cards.finding: Coverage remains overwhelmingly negative on governance, minimal on product. Financial media frames WTC as uninvestable for ESG-conscious institutions. Investigative pieces on White's conduct and board dysfunction continue. Coverage of product metrics, competitive positioning, or the restructuring programme is minimal relative to the governance narrative.
cards.tension: Media coverage amplifies the governance narrative but adds limited analytical value. The governance facts are known and priced. The restructuring programme represents genuinely new information that media has not yet fully analysed.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance Assessment
cards.finding: Governance structure unchanged; execution capability demonstrated. Richard White returned as Executive Chairman (February 2025) with 36% shareholding after mass board departure. No credible independent directors appointed. ASIC investigation active. However, management demonstrated execution capability: US$50M synergies 18 months early, e2open integration on track, and a restructuring programme that signals operational ambition. The paradox: the same founder whose governance creates risk is also the one executing the cost transformation.
cards.tension: The governance risk is structural and unresolvable while White holds 36% and the chair. But the 1H26 results demonstrate that poor governance is not (yet) translating to poor execution. This is the core tension in the investment case.

rows: No credible appointments since mass departure

cards.source: ASX filings; Board composition records; WTC 1H26 Results

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: No accumulation despite 50%+ discount; institutional exodus continues. White's 36% stake dominates the register: makes hostile takeover impossible, proxy contests futile, board independence illusory. ESG-screened institutional selling is structural, not sentiment-driven. Short interest elevated at approximately 3–4% of free float. No substantial holder has filed an accumulation notice, meaning no active manager is building a position at current prices despite the 50%+ discount to broker targets.
cards.tension: The absence of institutional accumulation is the strongest market-based evidence that the governance discount is real and persistent. If sophisticated investors will not buy at 50% below consensus, the governance risk may be larger than financial models capture.
cards.source: ASX substantial holder notices; Short interest data; Registry analysis

discriminators.intro: Four data points carry high diagnosticity for discriminating between WiseTech's competing hypotheses following the 1H26 results.

rows.evidence: CargoWise organic growth in 2H26. The single most important metric. Growth above 12% confirms Value Pack timing disruption (N1 strengthens); growth below 10% with no acceleration confirms structural deceleration (N1 fades, N3 solidifies, every scenario's implied price declines).
rows.currentReading: 9% in 1H26 vs 14% guided floor: below expectations

rows.evidence: Restructuring execution: 2H26 margin trajectory. Any incremental margin improvement in 2H26 vs 1H26 signals the restructuring is working ahead of schedule. Blended EBITDA margin above 40% in 2H26 strongly supports N2.
rows.currentReading: 38% blended in 1H26; FY26 guided neutral: awaiting

rows.evidence: July 2026 covenant test. Pass at ≤3.3× effectively kills N4. Tight pass at 3.4–3.5× preserves tail risk. Breach (now unlikely given synergy + restructuring buffers) puts N4 back in play immediately.

Board composition changes. Credible independent directors with institutional credibility would signal governance resolution. No appointments within 12 months of mass departure entrenches N4 risk permanently.
rows.currentReading: No appointments since February 2025 mass departure

discriminators.nonDiscriminating: Media coverage of personal scandals (priced in, backward-looking) • Social media sentiment (noise, retail-driven) • Analyst consensus target A$101 (stale, predates board departures and e2open acquisition) • Short interest levels (lagging indicator of known governance risk).

tripwires.intro: Three catalysts that would trigger reassessment of hypothesis weights, with specific thresholds defining positive and negative outcomes.

cards.name: FY26 Full-Year Results: The Definitive Test

conditions.if: If CW organic growth reaccelerates to 12%+ in 2H26, and blended EBITDA margin improves above 40%
conditions.then: N1 regains 3–5 percentage points. N2 gains 5–8 percentage points. Evidence-weighted price rises to A$63–66. Stock likely tests A$60–65 as the margin recovery narrative takes hold.

conditions.if: If CW organic growth stays below 10%, and margins remain at 38% or below
conditions.then: N1 loses 5–8 percentage points (fading toward 20%). N3 gains 8–10 percentage points. Every scenario's implied price declines as franchise growth assumption is reduced. Evidence-weighted price falls to A$51–54.

cards.source: Source: WTC ASX FY26 Annual Report and Appendix 4E

cards.name: Covenant Test: Pass or Breach

conditions.if: If covenant test passes at ≤3.3× debt-to-earnings
conditions.then: N4 (Reflexive Unravelling) effectively dies. Weight redistributes to N2 and N3. Market stops pricing capital raise risk. Stock re-rates as a margin recovery story rather than a distress story.

conditions.if: If covenant test is tight at 3.4–3.5×, or breached at >3.5×
conditions.then: N4 regains weight sharply. A breach triggers lender acceleration rights and likely forces an emergency equity raise at a steep discount. Stock could fall 30–40% from current levels.

cards.source: Source: WTC debt facility covenants; ASX continuous disclosure

cards.name: Board Reconstitution and Governance

conditions.if: If 2+ credible independent directors are appointed with institutional credibility
conditions.then: N1 (Franchise Intact) gains 5–8 percentage points. ESG-screened institutional investors begin re-entering the register. Governance discount narrows. Evidence-weighted price rises A$3–5.

conditions.if: If no credible appointments within 12 months of mass departure (by February 2026)
conditions.then: Governance discount becomes permanent. N4 retains structural weight regardless of operational performance. Institutional exodus continues. No re-rating catalyst while White controls board composition.

cards.source: Source: ASX board appointment announcements; Substantial holder notices

couldntAssess: AI tool readiness: Management claims AI replaces the output of 2,000 people. No external validation of internal tooling maturity, quality benchmarks, or deployment timeline exists
Restructuring execution granularity: Geographic distribution of cuts, phasing, and specific role categories not disclosed. US$80–120K blended cost per role is an estimate
Customer sentiment post-restructuring: Enterprise logistics customers have not publicly commented on whether 50% headcount reduction in customer service affects their WiseTech risk assessment
e2open integration depth: Cross-sell pipeline, customer overlap, and product integration roadmap remain undisclosed

Board deliberations: Reasons behind mass board departure not publicly disclosed. Whether directors left due to specific founder actions or liability concerns would materially change hypothesis weights
ASIC investigation scope and timeline: No public update on the investigation into Richard White's conduct. Resolution timeline unknown
gaps.analyticalLimitations: The restructuring programme is the largest single source of uncertainty. Management-sourced savings estimates (US$160–240M) cannot be independently verified until FY27 results. The AI replacement thesis is untested at this scale in enterprise logistics software. The 60% realisation rate applied to gross savings is a base rate from academic research on large restructurings, not WiseTech-specific. Governance risk is structural and unresolvable while White holds 36% and the executive chair.

crossover.description: 50-day MA crossed below 200-day MA as governance crisis selling accelerated. Both averages have declined steadily since. Stock remains deeply below both averages.

trend.structure: Three-phase decline: governance shock (A$127 to A$97, Feb 2025), sustained selling (A$97 to A$71, through Dec 2025), capitulation (A$71 to A$42, Jan-Feb 2026). The 1H26 results represent a potential inflection point with constructive price action on high volume.

resistance.method: Prior breakdown level and 50-day MA convergence zone

inflectionPoints.event: Break below A$70; capitulation phase begins

conditions: CargoWise organic growth reaccelerates to 12%+ in 2H26 (Value Pack timing, not structural)
Restructuring delivers 60%+ of US$160–240M gross savings from FY27
July 2026 covenant test passes at ≤3.3×, removing capital raise risk
AI tools adequately replace the output of 2,000 eliminated roles
Governance risk does not impair capital allocation or institutional confidence further
CW organic growth stays below 10% in 2H26, confirming structural franchise deceleration
Restructuring impairs product quality or customer experience before savings materialise
July covenant test is tight (>3.4×) or breached, reviving capital raise risk
ASIC investigation produces material outcome affecting White's position or company operations
e2open revenue deteriorates below the annualised ~US$550M guidance

riskAsymmetry.summary: The upside scenarios (N1 at A$81, N2 at ~A$65) carry 58% combined weight and sit 38–72% above the current price. The downside scenarios (N3 at A$48, N4 at A$21) carry 42% combined weight and sit 2–55% below it. The expected gain from upside scenarios exceeds the expected loss from downside. The asymmetry is favourable: the worst-case scenario (N4) has been materially weakened by the synergy and restructuring buffers, while a new upside scenario (N2) was created by a single day's disclosure.
riskAsymmetry.marketDisagreement: The evidence and the market disagree most on the probability that the restructuring programme creates a credible margin recovery. The market assigns negligible weight to this scenario (it was not in guidance until 25 February). The evidence assigns 30%. This disagreement is worth approximately A$5–8 per share and is the primary driver of the 25% gap between evidence-weighted price and market price.

conclusion.opening: The evidence-weighted price has risen from A$54.56 to approximately A$59. The market is at A$47. That is a 25% gap, widened by the 1H26 results, supported by fresh data rather than stale assumptions, with the primary downside scenario (covenant breach and unravelling) materially weakened. The current price sits 42% below N1's implied A$81, 28% below N2's implied ~A$65, near N3's implied A$48, and 124% above N4's implied A$21.

paths.label: Path A
paths.condition: CW organic growth 12%+, margins improving, covenant ≤3.3×
paths.outcome: N1 regains 3–5 percentage points. N2 gains 5–8 percentage points. Evidence-weighted price rises to A$63–66. The market begins pricing WTC as a margin recovery story rather than a governance crisis stock. Stock likely tests A$60–65 resistance. The 25% gap widens further before converging.

paths.label: Path B
paths.condition: CW organic growth <10%, margins flat at 38%, covenant tight
paths.outcome: N1 loses 5–8 percentage points (fading toward 20%). N3 gains 8–10 percentage points. Every scenario's implied price declines as franchise growth assumption is reduced. Evidence-weighted price falls to A$51–54. Gap narrows to approximately 10%. The stock goes nowhere while the market waits for FY27 evidence on restructuring.

paths.label: Path C
paths.condition: Mixed: CW growth 10–12%, some margin improvement, covenant passes comfortably
paths.outcome: Ambiguous. Weight shifts are modest (2–4 percentage points). Evidence-weighted price remains near A$57–60. The restructuring execution becomes the tiebreaker: if FY27 margins track above 42%, N2 gains decisively. Below 40%, N3 holds.

conclusion.closing: The case for upside coming out of these results is the strongest it has been since we initiated coverage. The restructuring creates a credible path to margin recovery that was not in the price. The one variable that could damage every upside scenario is CW organic growth. If 9% is structural rather than transitional, the franchise is worth less regardless of how well the cost-out executes. August tells us.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$47.10, the market embeds roughly 55–60% probability on Integration Tax and Unravelling scenarios and 40–45% on upside. The evidence assigns 58% to upside. The disagreement centres on whether the restructuring programme creates a credible margin recovery or whether CW organic growth deceleration signals franchise damage.
hero.skew_description: Two upside hypotheses carry 58% combined weight. N2 (Restructuring Recovery) at 30% is new, created by the 1H26 results. N1 (Franchise Intact) at 28% is weakened by 9% CW organic growth. N3 (Integration Tax) at 30% is the base case. N4 (Reflexive Unravelling) at 12% has been materially weakened by synergy and restructuring buffers.

position_in_range.note: Scenario-implied prices based on probability-weighted DCF and comparable multiples

next_decision_point.event: FY26 Full-Year Results + July Covenant Test
next_decision_point.metric: CargoWise organic growth rate; blended EBITDA margin; covenant leverage ratio
next_decision_point.thresholds: CW organic growth above 12% with margin improvement above 40% confirms restructuring recovery (N2) and franchise resilience (N1). CW organic growth below 10% with flat margins confirms integration tax (N3). Covenant breach above 3.5x revives unravelling (N4).

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence maps reported results against a probability-weighted scenario framework. It describes the direction and magnitude of evidence relative to market pricing. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company results presentations, and publicly available information. This report does not contain buy, sell, or hold recommendations.

_overcorrection.message: 5-day cumulative move of +15.4% exceeds 15% threshold — possible overcorrection
